description:
Advances: specled.verification

Deliverable:
User-directed scope expansion, carved by the orchestrator after the post-S1 merge gate (mix spec.check) came back red with 59 verification_command_failed findings. Root cause (independently confirmed by S1's verifier and auditor, and reproduced by the orchestrator in the epic worktree): lib/specled_ex/verifier.ex defaults command_timeout_ms to 120000ms, but this repo's tagged_tests verification command (mix test --include integration, ~62 files) takes ~245-320s on this host. .spec/config.yml sets no override, so every subject sharing that command gets verification_command_failed on timeout even though the underlying tests all pass (816 tests, 0 failures, confirmed independently).

verification.command_timeout_ms is an ALREADY-IMPLEMENTED, already-specced config knob (subject specled.verification, added in a prior commit "configurable spec verification timeout"). This ticket does not add new behavior or touch any code — it sets the existing config value in this repo's own .spec/config.yml, per direct user instruction to bump the timeout to 400s (400000ms).

- .spec/config.yml: add a top-level `verification:` section with `command_timeout_ms: 400000`.
- Regenerate .spec/state.json via mix spec.check (never hand-edit) — this run should now complete within budget and produce zero verification_command_failed findings.

Verification:
- Tests to pass: no new tests required (config-only change to an existing, tested feature). Confirm mix spec.check now exits 0 (or at least produces zero verification_command_failed findings) within the new 400s budget.
- Behaviors to demonstrate: mix spec.check completes without hitting the command timeout.
- Commands: mix compile --warnings-as-errors && mix test && mix format --check-formatted && mix deps.unlock --check-unused && mix spec.check

Allowed touches (this stage may modify):
- .spec/config.yml
- .spec/state.json

Out of Scope (files):
- lib/**
- test/**
- .spec/specs/**

Out of Scope (intent):
- No behavior change — command_timeout_ms parsing/enforcement already exists and is already specced under specled.verification. This ticket only sets a value.
- Do not touch any file outside .spec/config.yml and its regenerated .spec/state.json.

Merge gate:
- [ ] Code compiles cleanly (mix compile --warnings-as-errors)
- [ ] mix test passes
- [ ] mix spec.check produces zero verification_command_failed findings
- [ ] Scope check: diff touches only .spec/config.yml + .spec/state.json (mechanical)
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE comment on the ticket

Summary: Added a top-level `verification:` section to .spec/config.yml setting `command_timeout_ms: 400000`, the already-implemented/already-specced knob (subject specled.verification). Regenerated .spec/state.json via `mix spec.check` — the run completed in ~5m55s (within the new 400s per-command budget) and produced ZERO verification_command_failed findings (summary.findings=0, threshold_failures=0, 263 verifications executed, no "failed"/"timed out" claims). The regenerated state.json is byte-identical to the committed copy (it was already clean), so the commit carries only the config.yml change. Fast merge gates all pass: mix compile --warnings-as-errors, mix format --check-formatted, mix deps.unlock --check-unused. Diff scope is mechanical: .spec/config.yml only. No files under lib/**, test/**, or .spec/specs/** were touched.

title: Bump verification.command_timeout_ms to 400s to unblock merge gate
